message:
feat(core): add SYS_OPENAT2 + sys_openat2 wrapper for x86_64 + aarch64

Add the openat2(2) raw-syscall wrapper that the libc-side openat2 ABI
shim has been waiting on. openat2 is the extended openat introduced
in Linux 5.6 that takes a versioned `struct open_how` block (with
fields for flags, mode, and resolve_* membership masks like
RESOLVE_NO_SYMLINKS / RESOLVE_BENEATH) plus an explicit size to let
the kernel detect old vs new struct layouts.

This commit only adds the syscall plumbing (no public ABI export yet):

- SYS_OPENAT2 = 437 added under both #[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]
  and #[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")] (the syscall number is the same
  on both arches, unlike most of the openat-family numbers).
- pub unsafe fn sys_openat2(dirfd, pathname, how, size) -> Result<i32, i32>
  forwarding to raw::syscall4 with the standard syscall_result(ret)
  conversion to (positive_fd | -errno) → Result<i32, i32>.
- SAFETY contract on the wrapper documents that pathname must be a
  valid null-terminated C string and that `how` must point to at
  least `size` readable bytes containing a kernel-compatible
  open_how block — those are the kernel's preconditions verbatim.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
